# llms.txt — @lowdefy/modules-mongodb doc index
#
# Machine-readable index of the docs/ tree. One line per doc, grouped by
# module. Generated by scripts/gen-llms-txt.mjs — do not edit by hand.
# Re-run the script whenever docs are added, removed, or renamed.

## root

- docs/CONTRIBUTING.md: Docs Contributing Guide — Every file in `docs/` must open with a YAML front-matter block. The linter (Task 4) validates this schema at CI time.
- docs/index.md: Lowdefy Modules — MongoDB — A set of reusable [Lowdefy](https://lowdefy.com) modules backed by MongoDB. Drop them into a Lowdefy app to get authentication, user admin, contacts, companies, file attachments, audit events, and notifications without writing the YAML for each.

## shared

- docs/shared/app-name.md: App name scoping — Multi-app deployments share user, contact, and event collections across apps. Documents are scoped by **`app_name`** so each app sees only the data it owns.
- docs/shared/avatar-colors.md: Avatar colors — Modules that render user/contact avatars (`contacts`, `user-account`, `user-admin`) deterministically pick an avatar gradient from a shared palette so the same person always shows the same colors across the app.
- docs/shared/change-stamps.md: Change stamps — Audit metadata stamped onto every database write so we know **when** something changed and **who** changed it. The same shape is used across `events`, `files`, `companies`, `contacts`, `user-account`, and `user-admin`.
- docs/shared/event-display.md: Event display — Modules that write to the `log-events` collection store **per-app display titles** alongside each event document. The `event_display` var on each module is a map from `app_name → { event_type → Nunjucks template }`. The events module renders the template that matches the current `display_key`.
- docs/shared/secrets.md: Secrets — Master list of every secret read by modules in this repo. Bucket names, keys, and connection strings live in secrets so they stay out of version control.
- docs/shared/slots.md: Slots — Modules that ship list / detail / edit pages expose **slots** so consumers can extend each page without forking the YAML. Three slot vars are conventional:

## activities

- docs/activities/reference/vars.md: Vars — Var definitions are derived from `module.lowdefy.yaml`. Pass these via the `vars:` block in your app's `lowdefy.yaml` entry for this module.

## companies

- docs/companies/reference/vars.md: Vars — Var definitions are derived from `module.lowdefy.yaml`. Pass these via the `vars:` block in your app's `lowdefy.yaml` entry for this module.

## contacts

- docs/contacts/reference/vars.md: Vars — Var definitions are derived from `module.lowdefy.yaml`. Pass these via the `vars:` block in your app's `lowdefy.yaml` entry for this module.

## events

- docs/events/reference/vars.md: Vars — Var definitions are derived from `module.lowdefy.yaml`. Pass these via the `vars:` block in your app's `lowdefy.yaml` entry for this module.

## files

- docs/files/reference/vars.md: Vars — Var definitions are derived from `module.lowdefy.yaml`. Pass these via the `vars:` block in your app's `lowdefy.yaml` entry for this module.

## layout

- docs/layout/reference/vars.md: Vars — Var definitions are derived from `module.lowdefy.yaml`. Pass these via the `vars:` block in your app's `lowdefy.yaml` entry for this module.

## notifications

- docs/notifications/reference/vars.md: Vars — Var definitions are derived from `module.lowdefy.yaml`. Pass these via the `vars:` block in your app's `lowdefy.yaml` entry for this module.

## release-notes

- docs/release-notes/reference/vars.md: Vars — Var definitions are derived from `module.lowdefy.yaml`. Pass these via the `vars:` block in your app's `lowdefy.yaml` entry for this module.

## user-account

- docs/user-account/reference/vars.md: Vars — Var definitions are derived from `module.lowdefy.yaml`. Pass these via the `vars:` block in your app's `lowdefy.yaml` entry for this module.

## user-admin

- docs/user-admin/reference/vars.md: Vars — Var definitions are derived from `module.lowdefy.yaml`. Pass these via the `vars:` block in your app's `lowdefy.yaml` entry for this module.

## workflows

- docs/workflows/index.md: Workflows — Multi-workflow engine that lets apps declare workflow YAML, render entity-scoped action lists, and submit lifecycle transitions through engine-managed handlers. Submissions carry a **signal** that the engine resolves against a per-kind finite-state machine — authors do not hand-write status trans...
- docs/workflows/reference/authoring-grammar.md: Authoring Grammar — Reference for the action YAML grammar. Schema source of truth: `resolvers/makeWorkflowsConfig.js` and `designs/workflows-module-concept/action-authoring/spec.md`.
- docs/workflows/reference/exports.md: Exports — | ID | Path | Description | |---|---|---| | `workflow-action-edit` | `/{entryId}/workflow-action-edit` | Shared check-kind action edit page — universal fields + comment + signal button bar (`submit` / `progress` / `not_required`). No status selector. Addressed by `?action_id=<id>` | | `workflow-a...
- docs/workflows/reference/form-components.md: Form Components — Built-in field components for action `form:` blocks. The form-builder resolver substitutes each component's block config (with author-supplied vars merged) into the page block tree at build time.
- docs/workflows/reference/fsm-and-signals.md: FSM and Signals — The engine resolves every status change as a **signal** against a per-kind finite-state machine. A submission carries a signal name; the FSM looks up `(currentStatus, signal) → newStatus`. An unlisted `(status, signal)` pair no-ops silently. For the explanation of why this model exists, see [Sign...
- docs/workflows/reference/indexes.md: Indexes — The module does not create indexes — index creation is a host-app concern. Host apps must add the following indexes to the collections backing the `actions-collection` and `workflows-collection` connections.
- docs/workflows/reference/vars.md: Vars — Var definitions are derived from `module.lowdefy.yaml`. Pass these via the `vars:` block in your app's `lowdefy.yaml` entry for this module.
